name: Ziyad ibn Arib
title: Sa'idi Brave
shortDescription: A brave man from the Sa'id tribe who came to Karbala with a deep understanding of the path of Wilayah and sacrificed his life on the morning of Ashura.
fullStory:
Ziyad ibn Arib al-Sa'idi was a veteran warrior and a brave man of Kufa. Raised in a family that loved the Ahl al-Bayt (AS), he could not remain indifferent to Yazid's oppression and the disloyalty of the Kufans.

Before the day of Ashura, Ziyad left the tumultuous city of Kufa and reached Imam Hussain's (AS) comforting caravan.

On the morning of Ashura, the 30,000-strong army of Umar Sa'd under his command launched a heavy barrage of arrows against the Imam's 72-person camp. In this cowardly attack (the first attack), dozens of the Imam's companions drew their swords to defend the tents.

Ziyad ibn Arib was also one of these lion-hearted men who valiantly entered the fray. He fought the forces of falsehood with all his might and, after repelling many attacks, finally drank the cup of martyrdom in this very first attack.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى زِيادِ بْنِ عَرِيبٍ الصّائِدِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Ziyad ibn Arib al-Sa'idi.
